_metadata.title: ComplianceLint Interim Standard for EU AI Act Article 73: Reporting of Serious Incidents
_metadata.disclaimer: This is an interim standard developed by ComplianceLint. It will be superseded when CEN-CENELEC publishes the harmonized standard for AI incident reporting.
basis: EU AI Act Recitals 134-136 (legislative intent on incident reporting)
ISO 9001:2015 -- Quality management systems (nonconformity and corrective action)
EU Decision 768/2008/EC -- New Legislative Framework (market surveillance)
_metadata.awaiting_standard: CEN-CENELEC harmonized standard for AI incident reporting (expected Q4 2026)

requirements.title: Serious Incident Reporting Procedure
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST have a documented procedure for reporting serious incidents to the market surveillance authorities of the Member States where the incident occurred.
requirements.rationale: Art. 73(1) requires reporting of all serious incidents to the relevant authority.
required_content: Definition of serious incident per the AI Act
Authority contact information per Member State

requirements.title: Reporting Timelines
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST ensure reporting within the legally mandated timelines: 15 days for general serious incidents, 2 days for widespread infringement, 10 days for incidents involving death.
requirements.rationale: Art. 73(2)-(4) specify differentiated reporting deadlines based on incident severity.
acceptable_implementations: Incident response runbook with timeline-based escalation tiers
Automated alerting system with deadline tracking
Incident management system with SLA enforcement
On-call rotation with authority for expedited reporting

requirements.title: Incident Investigation and Corrective Action
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST perform necessary investigations without delay after a serious incident, including risk assessment and corrective action.
requirements.rationale: Art. 73(6) requires immediate investigation with risk assessment and corrective measures.
required_content: Investigation methodology and root cause analysis
Risk assessment of the incident and its potential recurrence
Verification that corrective actions are effective
